when_to_use: You need a simple holdout estimate before cross-validation.
data_needed: One row per observation with target and predictors.
statistical_checks: Leakage, target balance, time ordering, grouped rows.
sql_role: Create split labels and persist them.

when_to_use: Rows are independent enough to rotate train/test folds.
data_needed: Target, predictors, fold id or random seed.

when_to_use: A single CV split is too noisy for the decision.
You need an unbiased estimate after tuning many candidates.
data_needed: Outer folds and inner tuning folds.
statistical_checks: Overfitting to validation data, sample size.

when_to_use: The model predicts future periods from past periods.
data_needed: Timestamped rows, target, predictors known at prediction time.

when_to_use: Rows repeat inside people, stores, schools, customers, teams, or geographies.
Sample size is small or uncertainty in the metric matters.
Every tuned model needs a naive benchmark.
You need an interpretable comparison before ML.
You want a smaller formula from a large candidate set.
statistical_checks: Multiple testing, hierarchy, collinearity, validation gap.

when_to_use: You want to remove weak terms from a full model.
statistical_checks: Collinearity, sample size per term, hierarchy.

when_to_use: You want forward and backward moves using AIC or BIC.
You can afford exhaustive subset search for a small predictor set.
statistical_checks: Search multiplicity, adjusted R2, validation metric.

when_to_use: Predictors are correlated or many weak predictors matter.
data_needed: Numeric model matrix, continuous or binary target.

when_to_use: You want embedded feature selection with shrinkage.
You want feature selection but predictors are correlated.
You need probability modeling with many predictors.
sql_role: Create balanced folds and class rates.

when_to_use: You need interpretable nonlinear splits for a continuous target.
data_needed: Continuous target, predictors with possible nonlinear effects.
statistical_checks: Overfit gap, leaf size, monotonic plausibility.
sql_role: Prepare features and record tree rules.

when_to_use: You need interpretable nonlinear splits for a categorical target.
A tree is too deep or unstable.
statistical_checks: Validation curve, leaf count, rule stability.

when_to_use: One tree is unstable and variance is high.
You need a strong nonlinear benchmark with limited tuning.
data_needed: Target, predictors, enough rows for trees.
statistical_checks: OOB error, variable importance bias, grouped leakage.

when_to_use: You want lower variance and faster randomized split selection.
You need high predictive accuracy with careful tuning.
You need strong tabular prediction with regularization and early stopping.
key_parameters: eta, max_depth, min_child_weight, subsample, colsample_bytree, lambda, alpha, nrounds
statistical_checks: Leakage, early stopping split, class imbalance, calibration.
sql_role: Export feature matrix and fold ids.

when_to_use: You need fast gradient boosting for large tabular data.
data_needed: Feature matrix, target, categorical handling plan.

when_to_use: You need boosting with many categorical predictors.
statistical_checks: Target leakage in encoding, validation design.

when_to_use: You need a boosted weak-learner baseline.
Relationships are smooth and nonlinear but should remain interpretable.
sql_role: Create spline basis columns if needed.

when_to_use: You need automatic hinge functions and interactions.
Local similarity should drive prediction.
You need nonlinear boundaries or robust margin models.
You need a fast probabilistic text or categorical baseline.
data_needed: Categorical/text features or binned numeric features.

when_to_use: You have nonlinear signal and enough data to tune carefully.
Predictors are many and collinear.
statistical_checks: Explained variance, validation RMSE, interpretability loss.

when_to_use: You want supervised components for correlated predictors.
You need quick screening before heavier models.
You want wrapper-based feature selection.
sql_role: Store selected feature set per fold.

when_to_use: Categorical predictors need usable model inputs.
Missingness is informative or large enough to affect fit.
Target is skewed, bounded, or multiplicative.
data_needed: Continuous positive target or bounded rate.

when_to_use: Domain effects are conditional on another predictor.
False negatives and false positives have unequal cost or classes are imbalanced.
data_needed: Binary or multiclass target, class counts.

when_to_use: Minority class has too few examples for the learner.
data_needed: Training rows only, binary or multiclass target.
statistical_checks: Leakage from resampling before split, calibration.

when_to_use: The default 0.5 cutoff is not decision-optimal.
Predicted probabilities are too high or too low.
statistical_checks: Brier score, calibration curve, sample size.

when_to_use: You need prediction intervals with empirical coverage.
You need a transparent teaching grid.
The search space is large and not all parameters matter equally.
Model training is expensive and search space is continuous.
statistical_checks: Overfitting to validation metric, noisy objective.

when_to_use: Boosting or neural networks can overfit with too many rounds.
data_needed: Training and validation set or folds.

when_to_use: Different model families make complementary errors.
data_needed: Out-of-fold predictions, base learners, meta learner.
statistical_checks: Leakage from in-sample predictions, overfit meta learner.

when_to_use: You want a simple holdout-based ensemble.
You need model-agnostic feature impact after tuning.
You need to explain nonlinear model behavior.
data_needed: Fitted model, feature grid, validation rows.

when_to_use: You need local explanations for tree or tabular models.
A deployed model may degrade over time.
data_needed: Scored rows by time, features, predictions, outcomes when available.
statistical_checks: Data drift, target drift, calibration drift.

when_to_use: You need to decide whether a candidate beats production.
You tune prompts or model settings with statistical discipline.
data_needed: One row per output, prompt, model, item, evaluator score.
statistical_checks: Randomized item assignment, rater agreement, calibration, cost.
sql_role: Store evaluation table by prompt and model.

when_to_use: Every tuned model needs a readable decision record.
data_needed: Target, grain, predictors, split, metrics, assumptions, limitations.
